items.title: 'Neverness to Everness' Launches 'Convenience Store Tycoon' Collaboration with GS25 on the 18th
items.summary: Source: Perfect World©Perfect World'Neverness to Everness' (NTE), a supernatural urban open-world RPG developed by Hotta Studio under Perfect World, will launch a 'Convenience Store Tycoon' collaboration with GS25 starting on the 18th.
items.content:
'Neverness to Everness' (NTE), a supernatural urban open-world RPG developed by Hotta Studio under Perfect World, will launch a 'Convenience Store Tycoon' collaboration with GS25 starting on the 18th. The partnership will run until July 15, featuring a variety of events, including the release of six collaboration bento boxes and the operation of an offline semi-pop-up store.

During the event, users who purchase one of the six special 'Neverness to Everness' bento boxes will receive a coupon code for a 'Special Hunter Guide' and 'Chaos Dye.' By redeeming these coupons, users can randomly obtain either the GS25 collaboration profile frame 'Wonderful Life' or 800 Aethel (in-game currency).

A stamp collection event will also run simultaneously via the 'Our Neighborhood GS' mobile app. Users can collect stamps by purchasing collaboration products to enter a prize draw, with rewards including limited-edition merchandise such as acrylic keychains, special game coupons, and framed mood lights.

A semi-pop-up store commemorating the partnership will be open from the 19th to the 23rd at the GS25 Hapjeong Premium branch. The store will feature 'Neverness to Everness'-themed decorations and sell exclusive 'Convenience Store Tycoon' merchandise.

To provide visitors with a unique experience, a special promotion will run for five days from the start of the pop-up through the 23rd. Customers who purchase collaboration products on-site will receive tiered rewards based on their spending, such as mint bags, acrylics, random character and slide photo cards, and acrylic magnet clips. Users who verify their in-game Tycoon grade on-site will be gifted a coupon code for 50k Pons.

Additionally, participatory events like mini-games will offer cushions, acrylic stands, and stickers. During specific periods, cosplayers wearing collaboration outfits and the voice actor for the character 'Lacrimosa' will visit to interact with fans.

Meanwhile, 'Lee Hwan' saw a positive response from users following the release of the new character 'Lacrimosa' in the 'Version 1.1' update on the 3rd. The game continues to pursue unique partnerships, such as the collaboration with global automotive brand 'Porsche' announced during that update.

'Neverness to Everness' is available on multiple platforms, including its official website, PlayStation 5 (PS5), iOS, and Android. Pre-registration is currently open via Steam, the Epic Games Store, and the Samsung Galaxy Store.

items.title: Data digest: Pixel Flow hits $100m, May’s top games, Neverness to Everness, Pokémon Go, more
items.summary: There’s also more UA finance news and some stats about loyalty systems in this week’s round-up.
items.content:
Every Wednesday we break down the latest data, research and financial results into digestible chunks.

Read on for the numbers you need to know about without the fluff.

This column is sponsored by Xsolla , offering the #1 web shop solution for mobile games that generates more value, purchases, and revenue. Get started here.

Pixel Flow has topped $100m in IAP revenue since its release in August 2025, according to Appmagic data analysed by game developer Kavindu Priyanath .

Loom Games’ title has become just the third hybridcasual puzzle to surpass the milestone, according to the Appmagic data. With $105m in IAP revenue to date, the relatively recently launched Pixel Flow is hot on the heels of Screwdom ($120m) and Color Block Jam ($148m).

Hybridcasual puzzle games are performing strongly so far this year. Collectively, the top 10 games generated over $270m in the first five months of 2026, compared to around $340m racked up by the top 10 during the whole of 2025.

Worldwide consumer spending on mobile games hit $6.6bn in May, according to Sensor Tower estimates .

Global spending on games across the App Store and Google Play was up 2% MoM, with the US accounting for 29.6% of total revenue, ahead of China (iOS only) at 16.8% and Japan at 12%.

Boosted by strong live-ops momentum in China, Tencent’s Honor of Kings took the top spot on the revenue chart for the second month running in May.

Dream’s Royal Match jumped two spots to number two by worldwide revenue, while Century’s Whiteout Survival was a non-mover in third place. Wuthering Waves was the title showing the biggest MoM revenue rise for May.

Global mobile game downloads increased 5% MoM to hit 3.77bn in May. India accounted for 703m downloads (18.7%), ahead of the US (7.4%) and Brazil (6.3%).

Lessmore’s Arrows Puzzle Escape moved up two places from April to become the most downloaded mobile game globally in May, overtaking Hungry’s Block Blast, which dropped to number two.

Roblox moved up one place to number three, while Garena’s Free Fire and Oakever’s Arrows Go! rounded off the top five.

Officially released worldwide on April 29, Appmagic data suggests ‘anime GTA’ game Neverness to Everness has 9.3m downloads to date, and has earned publisher Perfect World just over $28m in IAP revenue.

items.title: Reroll Guide - Neverness to Everness (NTE)
items.summary: Check out this guide on how to reroll in NTE, who to reroll for, what free characters you'll get, and more.
items.content:
Unlike most mainstream Gacha games, Neverness to Everness has no 50/50 chance to get the limited characters. So every time you hit an S Rank on Limited banner, player gets the rate-up character.

This result in consistent and easy acquiring of limited characters, but less characters from Standard banner on all accounts.

At first two weeks after launch, there's only a single limited character to get: Nanally . Then there're the small roster of free character .

So the team building options are kind of limited, and S Rank Standard banner characters become more valuable.

Before deciding to reroll, you should learn what you are getting without the extra effort

This mean you don't need to reroll to get a team that's functional, you will have something that works. But reroll will help you optimize your start, and aim for either what you like or what's best , not just alright.

This method favors rapid rerolls, with each run from ten to twenty minutes.

Note that these ranking are not the exact ranking in our tier list, but this is basically an evaluation of what's best to start with on day one among these six Standard Banner characters

The absolute best picks, characters who has immediate high value, and best in slot in top teams.

If you didn't get neither Daffodill nor Jiuyuan , you can keep rerolling.

Very good characters, can hold on their own in most cases, and have decent teams. The only reason they are a tier below is because there are slightly better options that has immense value in previous tier

Not bad characters, they still function and can build a team around. But they are not recommended as target rerolls. They are slightly behind at launch, so imagine what happen after game release a long list of new limited characters

If you get one of these two, and you don't like the character dearly, you better reroll.

The new urban supernatural gacha game and ZZZ rival has just launched, and as any gacha fan knows, the premium currency doth flow freely on release.

Rather than spending all your Annulith on the regular cast right now, it's the perfect time to scrimp and save for when the properly powerful characters start arriving and shaking up the meta a little after launch. There are quite a lot of NtE codes to redeem, many of which include premium currency.

Add that to the usual lineup of events, exploration, and quests, and you should have a tidy little sum stowed away for any character you do decide to pull.

To redeem your NtE codes, you'll first have to complete the prologue section to unlock the mailbox and the phone menu. Once done, you can redeem your codes easily in-game:

items.title: Forecast: Fun Ahead — 18 Games Join in June to Stream on GeForce NOW
items.summary: Kick off the summer with 10 games this week, including the highly requested surreal open-world adventure, ‘Neverness to Everness.’
items.content:
June’s forecast with GeForce NOW : 100% chance of gaming.

GeForce NOW is lining up new adventures for the month, from big-name blockbusters to quirky indies ready for the spotlight. Members can dive into fresh worlds, squad up in new playlists and discover “just one more run” favorites — all streaming from the cloud, no downloads or upgrades required.

Eighteen games are coming this month, starting with the 10 games arriving this week with the highly requested Neverness to Everness .

Step into a surreal, supernatural open world in NTE: Neverness to Everness from Hota Studio . Reality bends, streets twist into impossible angles and the uncanny waits around every corner.

Play as an anomaly hunter drawn into a strange metropolis alive with anomalies, cosmic oddities and dreamlike encounters. Explore the city’s districts at street level or from impossible heights, uncovering secrets, side stories and hidden paths woven through the tangled skyline. Combat and exploration blend together as players move between quiet, eerie spaces and sudden bursts of action.

Every alleyway and rooftop hides something strange and new, from bizarre characters to otherworldly phenomena that reshape the environment in unexpected ways.

Streaming with GeForce NOW lets the game’s distinctive art direction and atmospheric lighting shine, keeping the city’s moody glow, deep shadows and supernatural effects crisp and sharp.

Whether wandering its streets on a big screen or checking in from a laptop, NTE: Neverness to Everness stays fluid and immersive — powered by the cloud.

Return to the Valley of Mines in Gothic 1 Remake , a faithful rebuild of the original experience that expands the world with more detailed questlines, additional nonplayer character routines and reactions, new traversal abilities and a fully modernized combat system. Step into the role of the Nameless Hero and explore a dangerous prison colony filled with rival factions, ancient magic, deadly creatures and choices that shape the journey ahead.

Whether revisiting a classic or discovering it for the first time, GeForce NOW makes it easy to jump into the adventure instantly across nearly any device, no downloads required.

And look forward to the games coming throughout the month:

In addition to the 16 games announced last month, 18 more joined the GeForce NOW library :

What are you planning to play this weekend? Let us know X or in the comments below.

items.title: NTE: Neverness to Everness Has Received Its Version 1.1 Update
items.summary: Perfect World Games and Hotta Studio have finally launched the Version 1.1 update for NTE: Neverness to Everness, called “Dreamwalk Corridor”.
items.content:
Perfect World Games and Hotta Studio have finally launched the Version 1.1 update for NTE: Neverness to Everness, called “Dreamwalk Corridor”. This update brings a solid mix of story progression, fresh content, and high-profile collaborations for players to dive into.

The update continues the game’s main narrative with a darker tone. Players return to Eibon, where things feel noticeably off. Nanally, still troubled by the disappearance and injury of her master Daffodill, follows advice to clear her mind, but instead ends up caught in a strange situation at the Phenomenon Welfare Private Service. This new chapter begins to unravel the mystery behind Daffodill’s betrayal. Players who complete it will unlock a permanent anomaly zone called the Corridor of Echoes.

Version 1.1 also introduces Lacrimosa, a new S-rank character with a unique presence. Often appearing half-asleep, she remains a powerful ally in combat, capable of creating objects, mimicking enemies, and inflicting the Nightmare status for heavy damage. Her debut is tied to the limited-time “Fading Reverie” board, where players can earn rewards, including cosmetics and her “Gilded Rhapsody” outfit, through cumulative pulls.

Beyond the story and character additions, the update expands the world with Sunward Island, a scenic new area featured in the Sunward Travelogue event. Additional activities like Fons Rush and the six-player Underground Circuit racing mode add more variety.

Collaborations play a major role this time. The Porsche crossover introduces the 918 Spyder and themed rewards, while partnerships with Opera GX and Beard Papa bring exclusive bonuses and real-world tie-ins.

Overall, Version 1.1 feels like a meaningful step forward for NTE, blending narrative depth, gameplay variety, and eye-catching collaborations into a well-rounded update.

If you want to find out more details about NTE, then make sure to check out our official review right here !

NTE will undergo version maintenance on June 3, 2026 from 06:00 to 11:00 (UTC+8). Once it's done, the all-new Version 1.1 "Dreamwalk Corridor" goes live. "Pink Paws Heist" will be temporarily unavailable starting at 05:30 (UTC+8) and will reopen once maintenance wraps up.

Login will be unavailable during this period, so please plan accordingly. After maintenance, restart your client to install updates. We apologize for any inconvenience!

※ Beyond the Rails: Prime Circle ends on June 4, 2026, at 04:59 (UTC+8). Be sure to claim any rewards before then.

● Eligibility: Appraisers who created their character before June 3, 2026, 06:00 (UTC+8)

● Distribution: Sent via in-game mail after maintenance. Remember to claim the items before they expire.

※ Maintenance may end earlier or later than scheduled depending on actual progress. Please keep an eye on our official announcements for updates.

PC: Close the game, reopen the launcher, and click Update.

Android: Launch the game and follow the in-game update prompt.

PlayStation®: Open the system menu, select NTE, press the OPTIONS button on your controller, choose Confirm Upgrade, then select Check for Update.

Available: June 3 (after update) – June 24, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Unlock Requirement: Complete the main story quest "Prologue - A New Destination - Head to Eibon Antique Shop"

Board Guide: The "Fading Reverie" Limited Board features the exclusive S-class character Lacrimosa, along with A-class characters Mint, Edgar, and Adler.

During the event, the "Fading Reverie" limited board features the "Tomato Duo" glider livery, the "Tomato Cruise" Regalia Pendragon livery, and Lacrimosa's "Gilded Rhapsody" outfit. Claim them from the Daydream Perks page by accumulating 50/120/200 rolls, or obtain them randomly through rolls before reaching these milestones.

items.title: Neverness To Everness Review – How Much Game Can You Fit In A Game?
items.summary: Neverness To Everness has you in the shoes of an appraiser in the supernatural city of Hetherneau. Free-to-play game that might have too much to do.
items.content:
Neverness to Everness is a game that I have had my eye on for a while. I was able to play through two betas , and now I get the chance to review the official 1.0 launch of the game. Does the now-released version of the game pay off the build-up?

Game Name: Neverness To Everness Platform(s): PlayStation 5, PC, Mobile Devices Reviewed On: PlayStation 5 Developer(s): Hotta Studio Publisher(s): Perfect World Games Release Date: April 29th, 2026 Price: Free-To-Play

In Neverness to Everness or NTE, you will play as the Appraiser for Eibon Antiquities. An antique store that also hunts down anomalies from the supernatural. Your character will have an eye to see things that most other people can’t. You can see and hear the effects of anomalies, which is why you get hired as an appraiser. A lot of the factions you meet will also add depth to the city of Hethereau. This game is basically mashing a lot of games into each other, and the city itself is a sandbox reminiscent of Grand Theft Auto cities.

The game loop for Neverness to Everness is focused primarily on what you can do in Hethereau. You can do story missions, side quests, fish, invest in real estate and buy apartments, become a cafe owner, and even more. Speeding through the story is difficult, as you need your hunter level to be at specific levels for certain intervals. You get hunter experience for anything you can do in the city. You can also grind materials by adventuring to locations in the city that unlock hubs for fighting mobs for materials.

Your main residency in Hethereau will be at the Eibon. You can adventure into the city and find phone booths to unlock fast travel spots. There are also sync towers that let you download data about the city, which will unlock more of the map for Hethereau. You can climb on buildings and even run a short distance up them. I do wish that you could actually run for longer since your stamina bar is longer, but it does make it quicker than just climbing normally. There are ravens throughout the city that drop oracle stones. They can be given to the Witch for extra experience points and currency.

The main currency for buying things in the city is known as Fons. These are given to you after doing most quests, though you can earn them in more consistent ways. I spent a lot of my time fishing to get Fons. When you catch a fish, you can sell that fish for fish scales and Fons if you give up city stamina. The rarer the fish, the better the return you get for that fish.

The best way to gain Fons is to advance the City Tycoon mode. This begins as a way to earn money by buying a coffee shop and investing in it. Once the coffee shop makes you enough money passively, you can unlock buying apartments. You can use the apartment to customize it or even invite your obtained characters to join you. The apartment does have some nice passives you gain from anomalies, so that also helps you in the rest of the game. Each thing you invest in has a way of helping you out in unexpected ways.

One of the last things you unlock from City Tycoon are bank heists. Though the bank heist in City Tycoon is not nearly as hard to plan for as Grand Theft Auto bank heists. It requires you to travel to the Pink Paw Bank Headquarters and get sent in through the entrance. As you travel around the supernatural sections of the bank, you can grab gold coins, cash, and other rare commodities. If you manage to escape from the bank before the authorities catch you, you will keep all the items you grabbed.

It is pretty easy to do the normal quests for City Tycoon levels to unlock. Unfortunately, there is one aspect not as easy, the Fons requirement. Once you get past level 14, you will need to get more and more Fons to unlock future levels. You will be forced to basically do the heists or fish all day in the game. There are some one-time places where you can get a good chunk of Fons, like banks that have vaults right out the gate, you can open.

NTE took a page out of the Genshin Impact playbook by putting team synergy based on character element reactions. They are just different and are utilized differently. The reactions are based on when the character changes. When you see a team building screen, there are elements that are lit up. The Elements in NTE are Anima, Chaos, Cosmos, Incantation, Lakshana, and Psyche. They will be shown in a circle. You want to light up the elements that surround the element to create good synergy. If they are connected, that means they will have a reaction when switched out.

Every character has a basic five-hit attack by pressing the normal attack button. Some characters have attacks that are different if you hold the attack button down. You can also press a button to use a skill. Each skill is unique to the individual character. There is also an ultimate button. You need to build your character’s energy to use their ultimate.

Luckily, NTE adds some complexity to their combat system by adding perfect dodge counters and parries. If you dodge right before you get hit, you can do a special attack that dishes out more damage than a normal attack would do. If your enemy has a wind-up attack, it will have a red circle. If you can press a normal attack button when the red circle turns yellow, you will parry it and stun the enemy for a time. You can also stun them by depleting their guard gauge.

Neverness to Everness is a Free-To-Play gacha game. It is worthwhile to look at how the gacha system works. So some things have changed between betas, and even since then, the gacha system has had small changes. You can use it like a normal system that skips an animation to multi-summon a bunch of pulls or a single summon. You can also use the unique way it pulls via the Fair system. The Fair system takes you to a game board with a character that represents you. Each pull is a dice roll. The dice goes from one to six. Based on the roll, it moves the character one to six spaces on the board. The spaces on the board are populated by treasure chests, character spaces, and arc spaces. Treasure chests have a high chance of being a B Rank arc or item. It has a slightly higher chance of being above B Rank if it is a golden treasure chest. Character spaces on the standard banner are random. It can be any character A Rank or S Rank. Limited banners have specific character spaces as well as random character spaces. Specific character spaces have that specific character and make it more likely that you will receive that character.

Pity in gacha games simply means a guarantee. If pity is 80, that means you are guaranteed to get a high-quality unit in 80 pulls. If it reaches soft pity, it means rates go up much higher. For instance, if you have soft pity of 60 pulls, once you go 60 multis without a high-quality unit, your chance of getting one becomes exponentially larger.

items.title: Review: NTE: Neverness to Everness (PS5) - Ambitious Anime Open World Gets a Lot Right
items.content:
The irritatingly named Neverness to Everness – which I’ll refer to as just NTE for the purposes of my own sanity from this point onwards – has launched into a crowded genre on the PS5.

Ever since Genshin Impact exploded during the pandemic, there have been several console copycats, all adopting the same free-to-play format, with divisive roulette wheel character unlock systems and a brain-breaking array of currencies.

This effort, from Perfect World subsidiary Hotta Studio – the team behind the rough around the edges Tower of Fantasy – sits somewhere between Zenless Zone Zero and Wuthering Waves on the spectrum. It’s an urban open world game with GTA Online life sim elements.

I’m just going to come out and say it: this scratches an itch for me.

I’ve always enjoyed games where the sandbox blends exploration with simulation, and NTE has you managing coffee shops and moonlighting as a taxi driver while you simultaneously work for an undercover anomaly hunting agency named Eibon Antiquities.

There are times where it feels like every pixel of this outing is paying homage to something else; its influences run deep, from the way its menus are presented like Honkai: Star Rail to quests which feel like they’ve been pulled right out of Control .

But I think it’s just about successful at combining all of these elements to create an identity of its own. It’s genuinely fun finding references at every corner of the built-up city of Heathereau, from a stunning replica of real-life Akihabara through to a bar that closely resembles STARRY from the anime Bocchi the Rock .

If you’ve played any of these types of games before, you’ll know the presentation can often be dry and stilted – but NTE pulls from manga, anime, and webtoons to create a schizophrenic style that feels like it’s constantly fighting for your attention.

It feels like a product of the TikTok era, where something interesting needs to be happening on the screen every 30 seconds.

But while it can be hyperactive, I do appreciate the overall artistic ambition of the dev here. In a similar way to K-Pop Demon Hunters , there’ll be moments where the art style completely transforms to represent the personality of a particular character in that moment – it’s a far cry from the static visual novel-style sequences in other similar games.

I’m actually into the story so far as well, even though it has highs-and-lows. As an amnesiac with superpowers, the game’s blank slate protagonist isn’t exactly breaking new ground – but thus far it’s resisted the temptation to go all-in on nonsensical lore, and so its launch day quests are actually quite interesting.

One sees you searching for a missing kid who communicates through sign language, and whose love of botany has got him tangled up with some possessed plants – literally . Another sees you infiltrating a high-stakes auction in search of a mythical object which will presumably have long-standing implications on the plot.

These quests all play out like short episodes, and while there are bad ones – like an unbelievably irritating romance arc involving an otter with an old-fashioned television for a face – I think they’re pretty much all a step ahead of other games occupying the same genre.

Unfortunately, the combat can’t hit the same highs as, say, Zenless Zone Zero.

items.title: NTE: Neverness To Everness | First Impressions Review
